title: Distributed Trace Sampling and Coverage
dataSources: Trace metadata, sampling flags, span counts per trace
spl:
index=traces sourcetype="trace:span"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as spans, dc(trace_id) as traces, avg(sample_rate) as avg_sample by service, _time
| eval spans_per_trace=spans/traces
| where spans_per_trace < 5 OR avg_sample < 0.01
description: Trace sampling rate and coverage affect observability. Monitoring sampling decisions and trace completeness supports tuning and gap detection.
value: Trace sampling rate and coverage affect observability. Monitoring sampling decisions and trace completeness supports tuning and gap detection.
implementation: Ingest trace metadata and sampling rates. Alert when sampling drops below target or trace completeness (spans per trace) is low for critical services. Report on coverage by service and env.
visualization: Line chart (sampling rate by service), Table (low-coverage services), Bar chart (spans per trace).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: APM/tracing TAs (Jaeger, Tempo, OTLP).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Trace metadata, sampling flags, span counts per trace.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest trace metadata and sampling rates. Alert when sampling drops below target or trace completeness (spans per trace) is low for critical services. Report on coverage by service and env.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=traces sourcetype="trace:span"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as spans, dc(trace_id) as traces, avg(sample_rate) as avg_sample by service, _time
| eval spans_per_trace=spans/traces
| where spans_per_trace < 5 OR avg_sample < 0.01
```

Understanding this SPL

**Distributed Trace Sampling and Coverage** — Trace sampling rate and coverage affect observability. Monitoring sampling decisions and trace completeness supports tuning and gap detection.

Documented **Data sources**: Trace metadata, sampling flags, span counts per trace. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): APM/tracing TAs (Jaeger, Tempo, OTLP). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: traces; **sourcetype**: trace:span. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=traces, sourcetype="trace:span". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by service, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **spans_per_trace** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where spans_per_trace < 5 OR avg_sample < 0.01` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Replay with a small load change. Compare with the OTel collector logs, pipeline dashboards, and health panels for the same labels.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (sampling rate by service), Table (low-coverage services), Bar chart (spans per trace).
grandmaExplanation: We connect metrics, alerts, and external collectors into our analysis so we can see coverage, backlog, and how work flows from probe to on-call in one place.
knownFalsePositives: Latency spikes during pool exhaustion, garbage collection, or upstream slowness. We compare with the APM UI and deploy history before a code-only theory.
